feature_gain_observations: The full uniqueness variant ranked ofi_300s_mean first, followed by four deterministic daily, funding-cycle, or weekly-cycle features.
Removing deterministic cycles retained ofi_300s_mean as the highest-gain uniqueness feature but shifted substantial gain to 900/3600-second volatility and return-normalization context.
The compact utility-margin variant ranked return_900s_bps first and achieved above-chance AUC on all five days, but every reported after-cost top-100, top-500, and top-1000 tail remained negative.
Feature gain is diagnostic and does not establish causation or a tradable signal.
critical_interpretation: Direct binary side-superiority learning raised the best pooled direction AUC from Round 34's 0.5245 to 0.5426, but no variant reached the frozen 0.55 gate.
The full uniqueness variant had the strongest pooled AUC but failed day-count and all three after-cost tail gates.

Removing deterministic cycles improved the uniqueness-weighted frozen top-500 mean from -9.41 to -4.02 bps, but worsened worst-day AUC and did not establish positive top-100 or confidence-ranked top-500 evidence.

The noncycle utility-margin +0.52 bps top-500 and +1.29 bps top-1000 diagnostics are not robust: its top-100 mean was -13.31 bps, confidence-ranked top-500 mean was -5.07 bps, worst-day AUC was 0.4546, and six of eight gates failed.
Bounded utility-margin weighting did not consistently improve discrimination or economics relative to average label uniqueness.
No tested representation established stable after-cost conditional direction on the consumed five-day calibration role.
rejected_responses: Do not select the noncycle utility-margin variant from its isolated positive broad-tail values.
Do not lower the 0.55 pooled AUC, daily stability, positive-tail, nonfinite, drawdown, turnover, or risk gates to manufacture a candidate.
Do not apply leverage because no unlevered executable edge was established.
Do not add more static-feature variants or seeds on the same consumed calibration role.
Do not claim maker economics, fill probability, or queue priority from best-bid-offer and trade data without a synchronized event-level Level-2 order book.
Do not claim ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT, cross-asset, policy, development, testnet, live, portfolio, or profitability evidence from this BTCUSDT calibration screen.

next_experiment.purpose: Measure whether observed order-flow imbalance, microprice, trade-flow, and depth-conditioned pressure carry stable forward information at any horizon before choosing a sequence architecture.
next_experiment.follow_up_if_signal_survives: Freeze one cost-conditioned multi-horizon causal sequence model, then evaluate on newly certified unseen BTCUSDT dates and separately certified ETHUSDT and SOLUSDT corpora.
next_experiment.follow_up_if_no_signal_survives: Reject this L1/tape alpha lane rather than adding model complexity.

research_basis.title: The Price Impact of Order Book Events
research_basis.use: Supports OFI and depth-conditioned pressure as short-horizon variables, not a 900-second profitability claim.

research_basis.title: The Micro-Price: A High Frequency Estimator of Future Prices
research_basis.use: Supports evaluating microprice displacement over explicit short horizons.

research_basis.title: Multi-Horizon Forecasting for Limit Order Books
research_basis.use: Supports measuring a forecast path across multiple horizons before selecting a temporal architecture; no performance is imported.

research_basis.title: DeepLOB: Deep Convolutional Neural Networks for Limit Order Books
research_basis.use: Supports spatial-temporal sequence learning only after the current data lane demonstrates a stable signal and the required book state exists.

research_basis.title: Order Flow Imbalance and Price Dynamics in CME Ether Futures
research_basis.use: Supports a signal-decay and placebo diagnostic hypothesis; its market, search process, execution assumptions, and reported performance are not imported.

research_basis.title: Bridging the Reality Gap in Limit Order Book Simulation
research_basis.use: Supports treating latency, event timing, impact, and execution parameters as material; its simulated results are not imported.
